summary: Matched several character select menu functions in src/melee/mn/mncharsel.c and advanced three larger unmatched functions by tightening local/static data layout, prototypes, and HSD animation/fog/audio calls. The decomp.dev bot reported +944 matched bytes: six new 100% matches and improvements to mnCharSel_8025D5AC, mnCharSel_8025EE8C, and mnCharSel_80266D70_OnLeave. Supporting changes split CSS data structs, moved BSS/static declarations into mncharsel.static.h, refined external function prototypes, and corrected GALE01 data symbol boundaries for mnCharSel data and SJIS strings.
what_changed: Implemented or partially implemented mncharsel functions that were previously marked with decomp placeholders, including mnCharSel_8025D5AC, mnCharSel_8025DAA0, mnCharSel_8025EE8C, fn_8025FAC0, fn_8025FB2C, mnCharSel_CostumeChange, fn_80263354, fn_8026407C, and mnCharSel_80266D70_OnLeave.
decomp.dev reported six new full matches: mnCharSel_CostumeChange (+524 bytes), mnCharSel_8025DAA0 (+148), fn_8025FAC0 (+108), fn_80263354 (+92), fn_8025FB2C (+36), and fn_8026407C (+36).
decomp.dev reported three partial improvements in unmatched functions: mnCharSel_8025D5AC to 92.43%, mnCharSel_8025EE8C to 73.83%, and mnCharSel_80266D70_OnLeave to 94.78%.
Replaced repeated magic flag value 0x10 with JOBJ_HIDDEN in JObj visibility operations and added a small sethidden helper around HSD_JObjSetFlags/HSD_JObjClearFlags.
Added includes needed by the new code paths: lbdvd.h, lb/types.h, and sysdolphin/baselib/fog.h.

Refined public prototypes in mncharsel.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures such as bool mnCharSel_8025DAA0(int door), void mnCharSel_CostumeChange(int door, u32 input), and HSD_GObj callback signatures.
Refined neighboring external prototypes: gm_80169238 is now declared as u8 gm_80169238(int), and lbAudioAx_8002411C now takes int.
Moved or declared many mnCharSel static/BSS globals in mncharsel.static.h, including HSD_JObj pointers, HSD_Archive pointers, HSD_Text pointers, state bytes, and the volatile CSSData pointer.
Split CSSIconsData by removing its embedded icons array from types.h and introducing a separate static CSSIcon icons[25 + 1] in mncharsel.static.h.
Split CSSDoorsData by ending the original struct earlier and introducing CSSDoorsData2 for the trailing data referenced as data2.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt data layout: mnCharSel_803F0DFC size changed from 0x190 to 0xF0, a new data2 symbol was added at 0x803F0EEC, and a former 0xA0 block at lbl_803F1000 was split into multiple SJIS string symbols.
Implemented menu/animation callback helpers that request/reset animations periodically using mnCharSel_804D6CEC modulo checks and HSD_JObjAnimAll, plus a fog callback using HSD_FogSet(GET_FOG(gobj)).

Implemented costume-change behavior using HSD_PAD_X/HSD_PAD_Y, gm_80169238 costume counts, duplicate-costume avoidance through mnCharSel_8025DAA0, mnCharSel_8025DB34 refresh, and lbAudioAx_80024030 sound feedback.

Implemented OnLeave resource cleanup and audio preload/update logic: clears SisLib, releases archives with lbArchive_80016EFC, updates pending_scene_change, computes a bitmask via lbAudioAx_80026E84, then calls lbAudioAx_80026F2C, lbAudioAx_8002702C, and lbAudioAx_80027168.
smart_moves: The data matching work did not force everything into one large struct: splitting CSSIconsData and CSSDoorsData into separate static objects aligned the C declarations with the symbol map and made references like icons[i] and data2.xf0[i] possible.
Replacing raw 0x10 with JOBJ_HIDDEN improved readability while preserving the underlying HSD flag behavior needed for matching.

The PR used narrow prototype fixes in dependent headers rather than leaving call sites as UNK_PARAMS, which gave the newly decompiled mncharsel functions typed calls to gm_80169238 and HSD_GObj callbacks.

Small inline helpers such as sethidden and repeated HSD animation request/stop patterns reduced duplicated code while still matching enough of the original instruction shape to improve difficult functions.
The costume duplicate check was isolated in mnCharSel_8025DAA0 and reused by mnCharSel_CostumeChange, making the do-while costume cycling logic clear and matchable.
The symbols.txt update split SJIS strings and a newly identified data2 block, showing that data-symbol boundary corrections can be a prerequisite for C-side struct and static-data matches.
decomp_lessons: When a static data blob refuses to line up, consider that one apparent C struct may actually be multiple adjacent objects; here CSSIconsData lost its embedded icons member and CSSDoorsData2 was introduced for the later block.
Update the GALE01 symbol map when struct boundaries are discovered. The code changes and symbols.txt changes reinforced each other for mnCharSel_803F0DFC, data2, and the SJIS string labels.

Do not leave local extern declarations inside a C file once static.h can own the file-local BSS/static layout; moving mnCharSel globals into mncharsel.static.h reduced clutter and improved consistency.
Typed callback signatures matter for matching HSD object callbacks: several functions became simple HSD_GObj handlers once their prototypes were known.
Modulo-timed animation resets, such as mnCharSel_804D6CEC % 40 or % 200 checks before HSD_JObjReqAnimAll, are a recurring menu callback idiom worth searching for in neighboring menu code.

For character select costume cycling, duplicate-costume avoidance depends on match type, number of open/valid doors, p_kind, sel_icon bounds, and costume equality; preserving that exact loop shape can be important.

Use project constants like HSD_PAD_X, HSD_PAD_Y, and JOBJ_HIDDEN instead of raw values when they still compile to the same code; this keeps decompiled code understandable without sacrificing match quality.
Float macro cleanup to shorter decimal forms can still match, and can make large static initializer tables easier to maintain.
naming_conventions: Address-based names remain for unknown or not-yet-renamed functions and globals, e.g. mnCharSel_8025D5AC, fn_8025FAC0, and mnCharSel_804D6CF5.
Public prototypes use descriptive parameter names where evidence is clear, such as int door, bool hidden, u8 idx, and u32 input.
The separate icon table is named icons rather than mnCharSel_803F0A48.icons after the struct split.
The newly split trailing door data is named data2, which is evidence-grounded but still provisional and not semantically descriptive.
A comment documents mnCharSel_804D6CF5 as the number of CSS doors with uncertainty: 'number of (open? valid?) CSS doors'.
Descriptive helper names appear where behavior is obvious, such as sethidden and isDuplicateCostume, while exact exported symbols retain their address-style names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0x30), PAD_STACK(4), and small local padding arrays to shape stack frames in newly decompiled functions.
Repeated original HSD animation sequence: lb_80011E24 to fetch a JObj, HSD_ForeachAnim with HSD_AObjReqAnim, HSD_JObjAnimAll, then HSD_ForeachAnim with HSD_AObjStopAnim.
Kept boolean/control-flow forms that likely improved codegen, such as lbLang_IsSavedLanguageJP() without explicit != 0 and if (!arg0) instead of if (arg0 == 0).
Modeled visibility with HSD_JObjSetFlags/HSD_JObjClearFlags and all-child variants using JOBJ_HIDDEN.
Used typed struct field access for CSS doors and icons, including mnCharSel_803F0DFC.doors[door].costume and icons[...].char_kind, after data layout corrections.
Used do-while loops for X/Y costume cycling so duplicate costumes are skipped until an acceptable costume is found.
Declared static/BSS objects in the file static header to align addresses and let C reference the expected storage classes.
Split a data symbol and SJIS labels in symbols.txt to align C initializers with actual data-object boundaries.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported a positive matching result: matched code increased by 944 bytes and overall GALE01 matched code rose by +0.02% to 35.54%.
Bot report listed six new full matches and three improved unmatched functions, providing the main objective evidence that the PR achieved its matching goal.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename data2 or CSSDoorsData2 in mncharsel.static.h.
Search for mnCharSel_8025D5AC follow-up work to complete the remaining 7.57% unmatched portion.
Search for mnCharSel_8025EE8C follow-up work to improve or fully match the remaining function.
Search for mnCharSel_80266D70_OnLeave follow-up work around mnCharSel_804A0BD0 or the provisional struct asdf.
Look for other menu modules using HSD_ForeachAnim request/stop sequences and JOBJ_HIDDEN helper patterns.
Search for PRs that refine gm_80169238 or lbAudioAx_8002411C prototypes further.
